selected_pocs.title: Untrusted Data Boundary
selected_pocs.goal: Implement strict input validation and sanitization at the boundary between user-supplied data and agent instructions.
selected_pocs.why_it_matters: Without clear trust boundaries, user input can manipulate agent behavior in unintended ways.
selected_pocs.rank_justification: Foundational - must be understood before any other injection defense.

selected_pocs.title: Input Schema Enforcement
selected_pocs.goal: Use Pydantic models to enforce strict input schemas for all API endpoints.
selected_pocs.why_it_matters: Ensures that only well-formed data reaches the application logic, reducing injection risks.
selected_pocs.rank_justification: Essential for ensuring data integrity and preventing malformed inputs.

selected_pocs.title: Parameterized Queries
selected_pocs.goal: Demonstrate the use of parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.
selected_pocs.why_it_matters: SQL injection is a common attack vector that can be mitigated with parameterized queries.
selected_pocs.rank_justification: Critical for understanding how to safely interact with databases.

selected_pocs.title: Output Encoding Practices
selected_pocs.goal: Implement output encoding to prevent cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.
selected_pocs.why_it_matters: Output encoding is essential to ensure that user input is not executed as code in the browser.
selected_pocs.rank_justification: Important for preventing client-side script injection.

selected_pocs.title: Rate Limiting Strategies
selected_pocs.goal: Implement rate limiting to mitigate brute force and denial of service attacks.
selected_pocs.why_it_matters: Rate limiting helps protect against abuse and ensures fair resource usage.
selected_pocs.rank_justification: Provides a basic defense against automated attacks.
